stories.headline: Optical character reader turns analysis into 4-month nightmare
stories.summary: USA Today's attempt to scrutinize Congressional expenses via scanned data was fraught with technical difficulties, leading to significant scanning errors and extensive manual corrections over four months.

stories.headline: Series leaves politicians with no one to blame for recession but themselves
stories.summary: A major investigative journalism project used spreadsheet analysis of government data to reveal that Connecticut families paid over 50% of their income in taxes, exposing wasteful federal spending and exposing that the Social Security Trust Fund relies on IOUs rather than actual reserves.

stories.headline: Projects at the San Francisco Examiner and the Lawrence Eagle-Tribune uncover (what else?) political waste and greed
stories.author_name: Dick Rogers, Brad Goldstein and Ed Achorn
stories.author_title: San Francisco Examiner and Lawrence Eagle-Tribune
stories.summary: Journalists describe their computer-assisted reporting projects, including a major investigation into San Francisco's AIDS programs using database analysis and a congressional campaign finance study tracking political contributors.
